topic: An artist discovers someone has tattooed their work
raw_output:
[story 1]

The gallery lights felt suddenly too bright, a harsh interrogation illuminating every brushstroke on the canvas. Elias stared, his breath catching in his throat. The intricate line work, the very essence of his latest piece—it wasn't just painted; it was etched. A sudden, cold dread washed over him as he traced a particularly delicate swirl with a trembling finger. He blinked rapidly, convinced the reflection in the varnish was playing tricks on his eyes. Someone had added something to his creation, something deeply personal, and it wasn't his. His hand instinctively flew to his chest, a tight knot forming beneath his ribs. He needed to step back, to breathe, but his feet felt cemented to the polished floor.

[story 2]

“Wait a moment,” Clara murmured, her voice thin, as she leaned closer to the framed portrait. Her eyes, usually warm pools of curiosity, were wide and fixed on the shadowed corner of the depiction. She reached out, her fingertips hovering near the surface, then recoiled as if burned. A sharp intake of air escaped her lungs. She pulled her hand back quickly, wiping a bead of sweat from her temple with the back of her wrist. The silence in the room seemed to amplify the frantic drumming of her own pulse. Every instinct screamed at her to demand an explanation, but her mouth remained dry, refusing to form a coherent sentence.

[story 3]

Marcus paced the perimeter of the studio, his movements jerky and uneven. He stopped abruptly before the easel, his shadow stretching long and distorted on the floorboards. He leaned in, his gaze sweeping over the canvas, lingering on a section where the texture seemed unnaturally defined. A low, guttural sound escaped him, a noise more animal than human. He pressed his palms against his temples, trying to physically contain the sudden surge of unease that was vibrating through his entire frame. He looked around the empty room as if searching for an escape route, his jaw clenched so tightly his molars ached.
